title: clymenum articulatum
intro: Clymenum articulatum, also known as Ground Ivy or Creeping Thyme, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mint family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Clymenum articulatum include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating digestive problems like indigestion and flatulence. Clymenum articulatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it easily accessible for those seeking its potential health benefits.
